description: Remove the enumerated tracker-ID/archived-vocabulary residuals that postdate or escaped the FGAP-136 de-jargon rollout and sit outside the delta-scoped comment-citation gate's reach: (1) comment citations of a framework-gap id introduced by the TASK-121 rollout commit into packages/pi-context/src/block-api.ts (4 sites) and packages/pi-context/src/rhetorical-criteria.ts (1 site) -- already-committed, so the staged-delta gate will never flag them; (2) the generated work-order commit message in packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/work-order-loop.ts, which emits the archived predecessor substrate's feature id in runtime output -- a generated-output surface neither FGAP-136's comment scope nor the citation-rot scanner's tool-description/error surfaces cover. Rewrite all sites to plain English carrying the same semantics per the FGAP-136 standard; enumerate the full class fresh (all non-test packages/*/src comments plus generated-output template strings) rather than fixing only the audit-enumerated instances.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh enumeration of non-test packages/*/src comments finds no internal tracker-ID citations outside the deliberately-excluded illustrative examples the FGAP-136 closure names
Generated work-order commit messages carry no internal tracker IDs -- the message template names the work-order id and plain-English purpose only
The rewritten comments preserve each site's semantic content in code-valid plain English
build, check, and full test suite exit 0
